colors module

colors.hex_to_rgb(hex_color)[source]

Convert a hexadecimal color string to an RGB tuple.

Accepts strings in the form #RRGGBB or RRGGBB. If the input is not a 6-digit hex color, returns (0, 0, 0).

Parameters:

hex_color (str) – Hex color string.

Return type:

Tuple[int, int, int]

Returns:

A tuple (r, g, b) with values in the range 0..255.

colors.rgb_to_hex(r, g, b)[source]

Convert RGB components to a hexadecimal color string.

Components are clamped to the range 0..255.

Parameters:

  • r (int) – Red component (0..255).

  • g (int) – Green component (0..255).

  • b (int) – Blue component (0..255).

Return type:

str

Returns:

Uppercase hex string in the form #RRGGBB.

colors.rgb_to_hsl(r, g, b)[source]

Convert RGB (0..255) to HSL-like integers.

Internally uses Python’s colorsys.rgb_to_hls which returns (H, L, S). This function returns an integer tuple that matches your UI needs:

  • H: 0..359 (degrees)

  • S: 0..100 (percent)

  • L: 0..100 (percent)

Parameters:

  • r (int) – Red component (0..255).

  • g (int) – Green component (0..255).

  • b (int) – Blue component (0..255).

Return type:

Tuple[int, int, int]

Returns:

Tuple (h, s, l) with integer values.

colors.hsl_to_rgb(h, s, l)[source]

Convert HSL-like integers to RGB (0..255).

This function expects H in degrees and S/L in percent. Values are clamped where appropriate, and H is normalized with modulo 360.

Note: colorsys.hls_to_rgb expects (H, L, S), hence the parameter order.

Parameters:

  • h (int) – Hue in degrees (any int; normalized to 0..359).

  • s (int) – Saturation in percent (0..100).

  • l (int) – Lightness in percent (0..100).

Return type:

Tuple[int, int, int]

Returns:

Tuple (r, g, b) with integer values 0..255.

colors.luminance(rgb)[source]

Compute a simple perceived luminance from an RGB tuple.

Uses weighted coefficients (Rec. 709-like) and normalizes the result to 0..1.

Parameters:

rgb (Tuple[int, int, int]) – Tuple (r, g, b) with values 0..255.

Return type:

float

Returns:

Luminance value in the range 0..1.

colors.blend(a, b, t)[source]

Blend two RGB colors.

Linear interpolation between color a and b by factor t.

  • t = 0 returns a

  • t = 1 returns b

Parameters:

  • a (Tuple[int, int, int]) – First color as (r, g, b).

  • b (Tuple[int, int, int]) – Second color as (r, g, b).

  • t (float) – Blend factor in the range 0..1 (clamped).

Return type:

Tuple[int, int, int]

Returns:

Blended color as (r, g, b).

colors.derive_theme_from_background(bg_hex, base_theme)[source]

Derive a full theme palette from a background color.

Given a background color, this function computes: - surface colors (slightly blended toward white/black) - border color - text color (pulled toward white/black but influenced by the background) - muted text color (between text and border)

The algorithm adapts depending on whether the background is considered dark (luminance < 0.5) or light.

Parameters:

  • bg_hex (str) – Background color as #RRGGBB.

  • base_theme (dict) – Base theme dictionary to copy and override. Must contain keys like text, primary, danger etc. Missing keys are kept as-is.

Returns:

background, surface, surface_alt, border, text, and muted_text.

Return type:

dict
